D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Optical Tweezers — Gradient Force on Dielectric Sphere

A tightly focused laser exerts a gradient force ∝ ∇|E|²; pulls high-index particles toward beam focus — Ashkin 1986 invention, Nobel 2018

Two competing forces: scattering force (along beam, pushes particle away) and gradient force (toward intensity max). For tightly focused beam (NA > 1) on small high-index sphere (n_p > n_m), gradient wins → 3D trap. Used to manipulate cells, DNA, viruses, single molecules.

Slide particle displacement to see restoring force.
